project.name: Reel Scripter
project.description: A minimalist web application for scripting Instagram Reels and short-form video content. It streamlines the workflow from idea generation to filming and archiving, providing a focused environment for creators.

user_workflow.primary_flow: User logs in (password protected) → Lands on Dashboard (default 'Write' tab) → Clicks 'New Script' → Redirected to Editor → Drafts content, adds 'Intros' (hooks) and 'Context' (research) → Marks status as 'Filmed' on Dashboard or Editor → Script moves to 'Edit' tab → Edited and marked 'Done' → Script moves to 'Archive'
user_workflow.user_goal: Efficiently manage the lifecycle of short-form video scripts from draft to completion without distraction.
key_interactions: Creating a new script via the Dashboard header
Editing script body, intros, and context in the specific Script Editor
Toggling script status (Draft/Filmed/Done/Archived) to move it through the pipeline
Quick-marking scripts as 'Filmed' from the Dashboard card hover state

project.tech_summary: Next.js 16 (App Router), React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL

architecture.overview: A Next.js App Router application utilizing Server Components for data fetching and Server Actions for mutations. It adheres to a minimalist design philosophy both visually and architecturally, with direct DB access in server components.
architecture.data_flow: Server Components (Page) fetch data via Drizzle ORM → Data passed to Client Components (Editor) → User interactions trigger Server Actions → Actions mutate DB → Path revalidation updates UI

key_components.purpose: Main hub for viewing scripts filtered by status (Write, Edit, Archive). Handles creation of new scripts.
key_components.interactions: Fetches script list, filters by tab param, triggers createScript action

key_components.purpose: Data hydration wrapper for the Editor. Fetches full script details, intros, and context items.
key_components.interactions: Fetches relations via Drizzle, passes initialData to Editor client component

key_components.purpose: Rich interaction interface for modifying script content. (Inferred from usage)
key_components.interactions: Manages local state, auto-saves via updateScript action

key_components.purpose: Encapsulates all business logic and DB mutations.
key_components.interactions: Called by client components to Create, Update, Delete, Login

entry_points.trigger: Clicking a script card or creating a new script

design_decisions: Use of 'force-dynamic' in pages to bypass build-time DB connection requirements on deployment platforms locally resolving DNS
Scorched Earth strategy for 'Context Items' updates (delete all, re-insert active) to simplify sync logic in server actions
Direct DB access in Server Components instead of an API layer for simplicity and performance
Password-based simple cookie authentication to avoid complex auth provider overhead for a personal tool

core.name: Next.js 16
core.why: React Server Components, App Router, efficient routing

file_organization.philosophy: Standard Next.js App Router structure with feature code inside `app` and shared utilities/db in `lib` and `db`.

important_files.role: Central file for all server-side mutations

conventions.naming: kebab-case for directories, camelCase for files/variables
conventions.structure: Colocation of page logic, separation of server actions
conventions.imports: Path aliases (@/*) used for cleaner imports

development_guide.working_with_this_codebase: Updates mostly involve `app/actions.ts` for logic and `app/[id]/page.tsx` or `components/Editor.tsx` for UI. Ensure `force-dynamic` is preserved on pages querying the DB.
key_patterns: All pages fetching data must export 'dynamic' = 'force-dynamic'
Mutations should use Server Actions defined in `app/actions.ts`
Database schemas are defined in `db/schema.ts` and require Drizzle Kit to push changes
Styling relies on `neutral` color palette in Tailwind for the minimalist look

common_tasks.approach: 1. Update `db/schema.ts` 2. Run db push/migration 3. Update `app/actions.ts` (create/update) 4. Update UI in `components/Editor.tsx`

critical_rules: Do not remove 'force-dynamic' from pages (fixes build errors)
Always use cascading deletes for script-related tables (intros, contextItems) to maintain data integrity
gotchas: The `updateScript` action deletes all related `contextItems` and re-inserts them on every save (Scorched Earth). Be mindful of this when adding persistent metadata to items.
